reasoning: Federal spending (FGEXPND) almost never decreases nominally year-over-year. Data indicates that in Q2 2025, FGEXPND increased by roughly $475 billion compared to the previous year. Furthermore, December 2025 reports indicate that DOGE "ceased to exist as a centralized entity" and massive absolute cuts did not materialize. To resolve YES, the Q4 2025 initial report would need to be at least $250 billion lower than Q4 2024. Given the massive upward trajectory in Q2 2025 and the disbanding of DOGE, this is virtually impossible. I am constrained by the 0.05 lower bound rule, representing extreme confidence in a NO resolution.
